feed.title: SAP Community - SAP ERP
feed.subtitle: SAP ERP Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Problema para exibir códigos de imposto (MWSKZ) na MIRO?
entry.content:
Recentemente, enfrentei um desafio curioso em um cliente. Após a criação de um novo código de imposto (IVA), percebi que ele simplesmente não aparecia na lista suspensa da transação MIRO. O mais intrigante? Não era só o novo código — outros IVAs já existentes também estavam invisíveis.

Começou então a clássica jornada investigativa: consultei documentações no SAP for Me, revisei todas as configurações standard recomendadas pela SAP (inclusive a Nota 2122460) e... nada. Tudo parecia estar em ordem. A dúvida persistia: por que apenas alguns códigos apareciam? Confesso que, até então, nunca tinha passado por isso em projetos anteriores — mas sempre há uma primeira vez.

Com apoio de um Expert ABAP, iniciamos o debug e — voilà! — encontramos a raiz do problema: um enhancement customizado no módulo de função HELP_VALUES_GET_WITH_TABLE.

Essa função é bastante utilizada nas versões anteriores ao S/4HANA para personalizar a ajuda de pesquisa — o famoso F4 Help.

A solução envolveu revisar esse enhancement e considerar a configuração sugerida na Nota 2122460, ou a substituição da função para F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST, já que a HELP_VALUES_GET_WITH_TABLE está obsoleta no S/4HANA.

Se você estiver enfrentando algo parecido, recomendo consultar as seguintes notas SAP:

  • 2122460 – MIRO/MIR4/MIR7: Códigos de imposto ausentes na lista suspensa
  • 3213274 – Substituição de HELP_VALUES_GET_WITH_TABLE

Abraços
Geziel R Cruz

entry.title: Problema ao acessar o Maintenance Planner
entry.content:
Estamos tentando acesso o Maintenance Planner pela url: https://support.sap.com/en/alm/solution-manager/processes-72/maintenance-planner.html , porém sem sucesso.

Existe algum problema?

entry.title: Test Automation Tool in post upgrade test
entry.content: Does TAT have reports on the results of all the test processes executed? If not, can integration with ALM provide the results automatically?

entry.title: test automation tool
entry.content: After allowing SAP to run post upgrade test automatically, do we still have to maintain variants for the system to run the tests. If so, what is the difference between manual execution and sap automation?

entry.title: Time Zone Change Impact on Timestamp Storage
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,
We are planning to update all our Linux/Unix application servers by using UTC time zone instead of EDT/EST


  • If data was stored in the database while the servers were set to EDT/EST, how will this information be displayed or interpreted after the servers are switched to UTC?
  • When SAP stores timestamped information in the database, does it use the application server’s local time, UTC, or another standard?
  • Are there any additional considerations we should be aware of?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Request for guidance on certification, test environment, and API package for our SAP integration
entry.content:
We are a Build SAP Partner and would like to request your assistance regarding the next steps toward the certification of our integration with SAP, and the options available to us.
  • Background and Integration Details
Our product, has been integrated with SAP ERP (on-premise) through a set of custom ODATA services implemented in SAP . These services include:
  • ZMM_EMPACT_UOM – Unit of Measure (GET, POST)
  • ZMM_EMPACT_ADDRESS – Address (GET, POST)
  • ZMM_EMPACT_CC – Company Code (GET, POST)
  • ZMM_EMPACT_PLANTCC – Plant (GET, POST)
  • ZMM_EMPACT_MATERIAL – Material (GET, POST)
Using this integration, we successfully demonstrated a bidirectional inventory-related flow from our application into SAP and back. 
Our application has an inventory capability and manages part sets and part instances, and we have established the following logical mapping between SAP entities and product entities:

Product Entity Name
 
SAP Entity Name
 
Address
Address
Organization
Company Code
Site
Plant
Unit Of Measure
Unit Of Measure
Inventory Component Set and Inventory Component Instance
Material
 
These are then pushed into SAP via the custom ODATA services and also retrieved back into our system. If the user makes a change in SAP on those entities, we would like to have ingested the changes from SAP into our product. Right now, this is done by checking the data from SAP and comparing with the current data from AMMS and if there are changes in SAP, we then bring them in AMMS.
 
  • Certification Goal and Migration Need
As discussed previously with the SAP ICC team, we understand that on-premises SAP integrations are no longer eligible for certification, and we are now looking to:
  1. Adapt/migrate our integration to a cloud-compatible SAP technology
  2. Define the appropriate certification scenario
  3. Obtain access to a sandbox/test environment for integration testing
  4. Achieve certification of our bi-directional inventory integration
     
  • What We Have Explored
Prior to developing the custom ODATA services, we explored the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, where we found the SAP Returnable Packaging Management package with the following ODATA/V4 services:
  • Material General Data
  • Material Plant Data
  • Company Code
  • Address
  • Plant
  • Storage Location
  • Unit of Measure
  • Serial Numbers
These services seemed promising, and we noticed that some were even available with sandbox access using SAP ID-based authentication.
 
Would it be possible, following further discussions, to create a dedicated API package in SAP Business Accelerator Hub for our specific use case and integration flow?
Our desired scenario centers around inventory management capabilities, including material, location, unit of measure, and plant-level data.
Is SAP BTP the most suitable platform for this kind of integration and eventual certification? If not, what alternatives would SAP recommend?
Could we be provided access to a test/sandbox environment to develop and validate our new cloud-based integration before submitting it for certification?
 
We are fully committed to aligning our integration with SAP’s current cloud strategy and technical standards and would highly appreciate your guidance on the fastest and most effective path toward certification.
Looking forward to your recommendations.

entry.title: SAP Query - Issue with missing fields on Selection Screen (MEBANF)
entry.content:
We are facing an issue with missing fields on Selection Screen of SAP query MEBANF after regenerate the program from SQ01 (Query->More Functions->Generate Program) where as result some of the fields from selection screen are not been displayed anymore (e.g.: Plant).

Objects involed (all SAP standard)
Query: MEBANF  (User Group: /SAPQUERY/ME)
InfoSet: /SAPQUERY/MEBANF
Logical Datavase: BAM

We already executed the following actions:

1. SE36: Verify selection fields from BAM LDB used on below InfoSet;
2. SQ02: Infoset /SAPQUERY/MEBANF  ->  Generate InfoSet and also Adjust (InfoSet->More Functions->Adjust);
3. SQ01: Query MEBANF  ->  Verify selection fields and performed Adjust and Generate Program

All above objects were compared (all properties, tabs, definitions) with other system under same version and SAP starndard query looks all the same, except selection fields from auto generated report 'AQZZ/SAPQUERY/MEMEBANF========'.

As result per attached screen shots list of fields from development system is lower them list of fields from Q&A system.

Any other way to restore missing fields from selection screen generate by Query ?

entry.title: Impact on implementing the note 3112362
entry.content:
Hi Team,We are currently in the preparation phase of our SAP S/4 HANA and are planning to implement the SAP  3112362  note - SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling in our ECC system. 

Before proceeding, we would like to understand the potential impact of implementing the note on our system.

Please help us with the below details.

  1. Potential impact of implementing this note on our production system, especially in terms of performance or stability during data collection.
  2. Whether this note should be implemented during non working hours to avoid impact
  3. Required pre requisites and dependencies 
  4. Any known issues or recommendations from past implementations of this note
  5. Any post implementation activities needs to be performed to complete the set up.

    Your support in clarifying these points will help us plan the implementation effectively.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Inquiry on SAP Support Scope for EHP6 ERP 6 on New Hardware Platform Post-Maintenance
entry.content:
We are currently running our SAP landscape on the following stack:

 

  •  

  • SAP Application: SAP EHP6 for ERP 6.0

  •  

  • SAP Kernel: 721

  •  

  • Database: Oracle 11.2

  •  

  • Operating System: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 12

  •  

  • Hardware Platform: Microsoft Hyper-V DIGITS R2 (now out of vendor maintenance)

 

We have come to understand that since our current hardware is out of maintenance, SAP may provide only limited support under such circumstances.

 

We are in the process of planning a migration to a new hardware platform while also upgrading our complete SAP stack. Before proceeding, we would like to get official clarification on the following points:

 

  1.  

  2. What are the exact limitations of SAP support when the underlying hardware is out of maintenance — specifically, which areas are covered and which are not?

  3.  

  4. Upon migration to a new supported hardware platform, what sort of full support is provided by SAP?

  5.  

  6. Are there any SAP Notes or guides that define SAP's support policy in such scenarios?

 

We are looking for this guidance to plan our risk assessment, migration strategy, and any necessary upgrades if required to maintain SAP support eligibility.

 

We appreciate your help in providing official clarity on the support coverage during this transition.

 

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: PO error MEPO305 even though there is no change on pricing procedure and routines
entry.content:
Unable to complete the PO 45****** Delivery Indicator checkbox or update anything on PO and getting an error "MEPO305-Condition sequence changed for item 000010 with follow-on documents"

 

Condition sequence changed for item 000010 with follow-on documents

 

Message no. MEPO305

 

Diagnosis: Condition sequence (KOMV-STUNR or KOMV-ZAEHK) changed for Purchase Order item with follow-on-documents. 

 

Pricing procedure for this PO is ZMI*** and we checked all logs of this procedure and requirement routines in it nothing has changes from very long.

 

PO created on 10.12.2024

 

First GR posted on 06.03.2025(March)

 

IR on 09.04.2025

 

Please note: unable to replicate in quality.

 

Please find attachments for PO error, Pricing procedure screen shot and change logs.

entry.title: currency conversion issue

Hi Team,

We have received a stock with reference to the material document 5025034885. 

please kindly refer attached document and let us know the currency conversation calculation for BSX and PRD values as per the attached document.

please kindly let us know if we need more information.

 

Regards,

Lovaraju

entry.title: How do we use multiple capacities to schedule correctly for forecasting?
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

I am trying to generate a capacity plan in SAP to evaluate the maximum required hours by day.

I am successful in creating the forecast, routings, and generating a capacity plan.

The issue is that SAP is pushing the start date for materials to the next day,

Consider an example:

The work center has multiple machines that do the same process so we intend to create one work center with multiple capacities.  However, the work center was configured with a few extra machines to allow for SAP to exceed our maximums (so it wouldn’t push jobs to the next day)

The work center is scheduled based on machine capacity

The routing is configured to allow the system to split the order to take advantage of the extra machines.

Material 4 has a forecast quantity of 3836 loaded on 10/21.

MS01 is run:

Planned order 76641 is generated:

However, the production start date is 10/22/25.

The total required hours for 10/21 for the specific work center across all materials is as follows:

 

Date

Material

Total Forecast

Hours

10/21/2025

1

243

2

10/21/2025

2

306

2

10/21/2025

3

172

1

10/21/2025

4

3836

31

10/21/2025

5

7107

58

10/21/2025

6

1927

16

10/21/2025

7

7286

59

10/21/2025

8

6

0

10/21/2025

9

16

0

10/21/2025

10

10

0

10/21/2025

11

28

0

 

My understanding is that the issue may be because planned orders won’t utilize splitting for capacity.  So how do we allow the system to plan and schedule for equipment with multiple capacities to ensure an accurate maximum demand?

entry.title: Withholding Tax is calculated on payments and Downpayment Separately
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

I hope you’re doing well.

 

We’re encountering an inconsistency in withholding tax calculation:

 

  •  

  • Threshold rule: For amounts up to ₹X→ 0% tax. For amounts above → 10% tax.

  •  

  • The down‑payments from vendors are being taxed separately and not aggregated with full payment amounts.

  •  

  • In contrast, regular invoice payments follow the correct threshold logic.

 

This discrepancy between down‑payments and invoice payments is causing incorrect tax deductions.

 

Could you please advise on:

 

  1.  

  2. Correct configuration for threshold-based tax accumulation across both payment types?

  3.  

  4. Settings or transaction keys to ensure down‑payments and invoices are treated in a single accumulation cycle?

  5.  

  6. Any known workarounds or patches to align the behavior?

 

Your guidance on aligning the issue across both transaction types would be greatly appreciated.

 

Thanks in advance for your support!

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Is there a way to prevent a user from entering the same transaction more than twice?
entry.content:
Dear community

Is there a way to prevent a user from entering the same transaction more than twice?

Thank you for your help.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How To Maintain Custom Form (PDF version) By Default For Inspection Report
entry.content:

Could you please help to know how to maintain custom PDF form by default for inspection report. Could you please suggest path. Currently system is showing standard form. Please refer below screenshot 

Regards,

Shree

entry.title: Is it safe to activate Business Function LOG_EAM_CI_7 in a production environment?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are planning to activate the Business Function LOG_EAM_CI_7 (Enterprise Asset Management Part 7) via standard configuration in SPRO.

Before proceeding, we would like to know if activating this Business Function in the production system — or in any environment — could potentially impact other existing processes or custom developments.

We are aware that once activated, this Business Function cannot be reversed, except by restoring a system snapshot or backup. Therefore, we are being extra cautious.

Does anyone have experience activating this BF? Are there any known issues, side effects, or dependencies we should be aware of?

Thanks in advance for your guidance and insights!

entry.title: Want to Process the Sales orders against the particular MRP only
entry.content:
Currently, during Sales Order (SO) creation in SAP, the system automatically selects the first available MRP (based on date/month) and deducts quantity from it. This leads to incorrect stock consumption and affects Raw Material (RM) planning.

We require the ability to manually select the MRP record (product-wise, month-wise, or project-wise) during SO creation. The system should then consume quantity only from the selected MRP and not affect any other MRP record. This is necessary to ensure accurate RM requirement planning and avoid consumption mismatches.

entry.title: Inspection characteristics not carrying over to the inspection lot
entry.content:
I'm stumped. I have a part that is assigned to an inspection plan. The inspection plan has several inspection characteristics. When looking at the inspection lot history I see the last 5 or so all have auto usage decision to accept. When I look at these lots in QE09, i see no inspection characteristics recorded.

Here are some key details on each piece of this puzzle:

  • Inspection Plan
    • valid from Jan 2019 until 29999
    • Usage: Goods receipt 5
    • Status Released/approved
    • There is a 100% inspection sampling plan set up on this
    • The part number is in the material assignment list
    • There is one operation with 4 qualitative inspection characteristics on them.
      • I checked one of the Inspection characteristics
        • Shows is a Required Characteristic in the results confirmation section.
        • A sampling procedure is checked for this though.
        • Characteristic is released and valid since early last year
  • Material master for the part
    • inspection type is set up, it has the standard goods receipt inspection type with preferred inspection type checked/active checked
    • Automatic UD is checked
  • Inspection lot information (from QE09)
    • created May 2025
    • assigned to the inspection plan described above
    • accepted by auto UD
    • no inspection characteristic data

does anyone know why the inspection characteristics are not carrying over to the inspection lot?

entry.title: Relatório de itens cadastrados na LPK1
entry.content:
Boa tarde,

Saberiam informar se há algum relatório ou transação que eu possa consultar todos os itens cadastrados na LPK1?

Grato

entry.title: How to write a PCR for Offcycle Medical claim Income Tax calculation.
entry.content:
@Dear Team,

We have a requirement where, currently in the system, if a medical claim is provided to an employee in the off-cycle payroll, the income tax calculation takes place during the regular payroll run. However, the new requirement is that the income tax should be calculated in the off-cycle payroll itself when the medical claim is processed. For this purpose, I need to write a PCR. Kindly help me with the PCR logic for this scenario.
